label: AWS AI Services & Real-World Use Cases — Part A
topic: Domain 1: Fundamentals of AI and ML

questions.stem: What is Amazon Rekognition?

options.text: A pre-trained deep learning service for computer vision that works with images and videos including streaming video
A text translation service
A speech-to-text transcription service
A database query optimization service

explanation.correct: Amazon Rekognition is a pre-trained deep learning service for computer vision. It handles images and videos (including streaming), providing face recognition, object detection, text detection, and content moderation.

distractors.B: Text translation is Amazon Translate, not Rekognition.
distractors.C: Speech-to-text is Amazon Transcribe, not Rekognition.
distractors.D: Database optimization is unrelated. Rekognition processes visual content.

questions.stem: What does Amazon Textract do beyond basic optical character recognition?

options.text: It extracts text, handwriting, forms, and tabular data from scanned documents
It only converts typed text from images
It translates extracted text into other languages
It generates new documents from templates

explanation.correct: Amazon Textract goes beyond basic OCR by extracting text, handwriting, forms, and tabular data from scanned documents. It understands document structure, not just character recognition.

distractors.B: Textract handles handwriting and structured forms too, not just typed text.
distractors.C: Translation is Amazon Translate. Textract extracts content from documents.
distractors.D: Document generation is a different function. Textract reads and extracts from existing documents.

questions.stem: What does Amazon Comprehend provide as a natural language processing service?

options.text: It discovers insights and relationships in text, including sentiment analysis and PII detection
It generates images from text descriptions
It converts text to speech
It trains custom deep learning models from scratch

explanation.correct: Amazon Comprehend is an NLP service that discovers insights in text — sentiment analysis, entity extraction, PII detection. It's pre-trained to find names, addresses, emails, phone numbers, and credit card numbers.

distractors.B: Image generation from text is done by models like Stable Diffusion/DALL-E, not Comprehend.
distractors.C: Text-to-speech is Amazon Polly. Comprehend analyzes text for insights.
distractors.D: Comprehend is pre-trained. Custom model training from scratch uses SageMaker.

questions.stem: What technology powers Amazon Lex?

options.text: The same technology that powers Amazon Alexa — for building voice and text interfaces to engage with customers
The same technology as Amazon S3 for data storage
Basic keyword matching without any AI
Image recognition technology from Rekognition

explanation.correct: Amazon Lex uses the same technology powering Alexa devices. It builds voice and text conversational interfaces for chatbots and interactive voice response systems.

distractors.B: S3 is storage. Lex provides conversational AI capabilities.
distractors.C: Lex uses NLP and ML, not simple keyword matching. It understands natural language intent.
distractors.D: Rekognition handles images. Lex handles conversational language interfaces.

questions.stem: What does Amazon Polly do?

options.text: Turns text into natural-sounding speech in dozens of languages using deep learning
Converts speech to text transcriptions
Translates text between languages
Analyzes text for sentiment

explanation.correct: Amazon Polly uses deep learning to synthesize natural-sounding human speech from text in dozens of languages. Use cases include converting articles to audio and IVR system prompts.

distractors.B: Speech-to-text is Amazon Transcribe. Polly does the opposite: text-to-speech.
distractors.C: Translation is Amazon Translate. Polly converts text to spoken audio.
distractors.D: Sentiment analysis is Amazon Comprehend. Polly generates speech from text.

questions.stem: Which capabilities does Amazon Rekognition provide? (Select THREE)

options.text: Face recognition — verify identity by comparing images against reference photos
Object detection and labeling — make image/video libraries searchable
Content moderation — detect and filter explicit, inappropriate, and violent content
Text translation — convert detected text to other languages
Model training — train custom deep learning models from scratch
Data storage — store images in a managed database

explanation.correct: Rekognition provides face recognition (identity verification), object detection/labeling (searchable libraries), and content moderation (detect/filter explicit, inappropriate, violent content and flag for human review).

distractors.D: Translation is Amazon Translate. Rekognition detects text but doesn't translate it.
distractors.E: Rekognition is pre-trained. Custom training from scratch uses SageMaker.
distractors.F: S3 stores images. Rekognition processes them for recognition and detection.

questions.stem: Which AWS services handle language and text processing? (Select THREE)

options.text: Amazon Comprehend — NLP for sentiment analysis and PII detection
Amazon Transcribe — automatic speech recognition for 100+ languages
Amazon Translate — fluent translation between 75 different languages
Amazon Rekognition — image and video analysis
Amazon Polly — text to speech synthesis
Amazon S3 — object storage

explanation.correct: Text/language services: Comprehend (NLP/sentiment/PII), Transcribe (speech-to-text, 100+ languages), and Translate (text translation, 75 languages). These are pre-trained and API-accessible.

distractors.D: Rekognition processes visual content (images/video), not text/language.
distractors.E: Polly generates speech FROM text (output is audio), making it speech not text processing.
distractors.F: S3 is storage infrastructure, not a language processing service.

questions.stem: What do Amazon Personalize and Amazon Kendra provide? (Select TWO)

options.text: Personalize — automatically generates personalized recommendations for customers based on their behavior
Kendra — intelligent enterprise search using NLP to understand natural language questions
Personalize — trains custom computer vision models
Kendra — generates marketing copy for products
Both services require building custom models from scratch

explanation.correct: Personalize generates personalized recommendations (e.g., 'You might also like') and segments customers. Kendra provides intelligent search using NLP to understand questions and return relevant results.

distractors.C: Personalize handles recommendations, not computer vision. Rekognition handles CV.
distractors.D: Kendra searches enterprise content. Content generation is handled by Bedrock/generative AI.
distractors.E: Both are pre-built, managed AI services. No custom model building is required.

questions.stem: What does Amazon Fraud Detector provide? (Select TWO)

options.text: Identifies potentially fraudulent online activities such as payment fraud and fake accounts
Provides pre-trained models for online transactions, product reviews, new accounts, and account takeovers
Replaces all human fraud investigators
Only works with cryptocurrency transactions
Requires training a model from scratch for each use case

explanation.correct: Amazon Fraud Detector identifies fraudulent online activities and provides pre-trained data models for detecting fraud in transactions, reviews, payments, new accounts, and account takeovers.

distractors.C: It assists fraud detection but doesn't replace human investigators for complex cases.
distractors.D: It works with various online transaction types, not limited to cryptocurrency.
distractors.E: It comes with pre-trained models. No from-scratch training is required.

questions.stem: When should you use the Amazon SageMaker family of services instead of pre-built AI services? (Select TWO)

options.text: When you need more customized ML models that go beyond prebuilt functionalities
When you need custom workflows for data preparation, training, and deployment
When a simple API call to a pre-trained service meets all requirements
When you only need to detect PII in text
When you only need basic sentiment analysis

explanation.correct: Use SageMaker when you need custom models beyond prebuilt services, or custom workflows (data prep, parallel training, deployment, real-time endpoints). SageMaker provides full ML lifecycle control.

distractors.C: If a pre-built service API meets needs, use it directly. SageMaker is for when you need more control.
distractors.D: PII detection is handled by Amazon Comprehend. No need for custom SageMaker models.
distractors.E: Basic sentiment analysis is a Comprehend capability. SageMaker is for more complex custom needs.

questions.scenario: A security company needs to verify employee identity at building entrances by comparing live camera images against their employee badge photos.
questions.stem: Which AWS service can verify identity by comparing a live face image against a reference image?

options.text: Amazon Rekognition — face recognition that verifies identity by comparing images with reference photos like badges or licenses
Amazon Comprehend — analyzes the text on the badge
Amazon Textract — extracts text from the badge image
Amazon Polly — announces the employee's name

explanation.correct: Rekognition's face recognition can verify someone's identity by comparing their image with a reference image (badge, driver's license). It returns a confidence score for the match.

distractors.B: Comprehend processes text, not images. Face verification requires visual comparison.
distractors.C: Textract extracts text from documents. Face verification needs face comparison, not text extraction.
distractors.D: Polly generates speech. It doesn't compare faces for identity verification.

questions.scenario: A data engineering team is collecting emails to train a spam detection model. They need to identify and remove personal information like names, addresses, and credit card numbers before using the data for training.
questions.stem: Which AWS service can detect PII in text data?

options.text: Amazon Comprehend — pre-trained to find PII including names, addresses, emails, phone numbers, and credit card numbers
Amazon Rekognition — detects faces in the email
Amazon Polly — reads the email aloud to check for PII
Amazon S3 — scans stored objects for sensitive data

explanation.correct: Amazon Comprehend is pre-trained to find PII in text — names, addresses, emails, phone numbers, credit card numbers. It returns entities with confidence scores for threshold-based removal.

distractors.B: Rekognition processes images/video. PII in text requires NLP (Comprehend).
distractors.C: Polly converts text to speech. It doesn't analyze content for PII.
distractors.D: S3 stores data. Amazon Macie scans S3 for sensitive data, but Comprehend handles text PII detection.

questions.scenario: A company's call center receives thousands of calls daily. Customers are frustrated with the touch-tone menu system and often just press 0 to reach an agent. The company wants customers to simply speak their request.
questions.stem: Which AWS service enables natural language voice interaction for their IVR system?

options.text: Amazon Lex — builds voice and text conversational interfaces using NLP, letting customers speak instead of pressing buttons
Amazon Polly — converts the menu options to speech
Amazon Transcribe — records all customer calls
Amazon Translate — translates customer speech to English

explanation.correct: Amazon Lex builds conversational interfaces using NLP. DoorDash used this exact approach — replacing touch-tone IVR with natural language processing so customers can just speak their requests.

distractors.B: Polly generates speech (text-to-speech). Lex understands natural language input from users.
distractors.C: Transcribe converts speech to text. Lex understands intent and routes conversations.
distractors.D: Translate handles language conversion. The issue is understanding customer intent, not language barriers.

questions.scenario: An e-commerce company wants to show a 'You might also like' section with personalized product recommendations based on each customer's browsing and purchase history.
questions.stem: Which AWS service automatically generates these personalized recommendations?

options.text: Amazon Personalize — automatically generates personalized recommendations and can segment customers by preferences
Amazon Comprehend — analyzes product descriptions for sentiment
Amazon Kendra — searches enterprise documents for product info
Amazon Rekognition — recognizes products in images

explanation.correct: Amazon Personalize generates personalized recommendations for industries like retail and entertainment. It powers 'You might also like' sections and can segment customers for targeted marketing campaigns.

distractors.B: Comprehend analyzes text sentiment. Personalize builds recommendation models from user behavior.
distractors.C: Kendra searches enterprise content. Personalize specifically handles recommendation generation.
distractors.D: Rekognition identifies objects in images. Personalize recommends products based on behavior patterns.

questions.scenario: A customer support platform needs to enable real-time chat between agents speaking English and customers speaking Spanish, with the translation happening automatically within the chat application.
questions.stem: Which AWS service provides real-time text translation for this chat use case?

options.text: Amazon Translate — fluently translates between 75 languages using a neural network that considers full sentence context
Amazon Comprehend — understands the sentiment of each message
Amazon Lex — builds the chatbot interface
Amazon Transcribe — converts the chat to audio

explanation.correct: Amazon Translate fluently translates text between 75 languages using a neural network that considers the entire sentence context (not just word-by-word). Real-time chat translation is a key use case.

distractors.B: Comprehend analyzes text meaning. Translation requires Amazon Translate specifically.
distractors.C: Lex builds conversational interfaces. Translation between languages is Translate's function.
distractors.D: Transcribe converts speech to text. Chat is already text — it needs translation, not transcription.

questions.scenario: A team is studying how enterprises use AI on AWS to solve real business problems.
questions.stem: Which are real-world examples of AI solving business problems on AWS? (Select THREE)

options.text: MasterCard tripled fraudulent transaction detection and reduced false positives by 10x using SageMaker
Laredo Petroleum monitors oil well sensors in real-time with SageMaker ML to detect leaks and prevent environmental issues
Booking.com uses SageMaker for booking recommendations and RAG-powered AI Trip Planner for natural language planning
All companies use AI only for customer service chatbots
AI on AWS is only suitable for technology companies
AI cannot be applied to physical operations like oil wells

explanation.correct: Real examples: MasterCard (fraud detection), Laredo Petroleum (sensor monitoring/leak detection), Booking.com (recommendations + RAG Trip Planner). AI applies across finance, energy, and travel.

distractors.D: Companies use AI for fraud detection, sensor monitoring, recommendations, and many more applications beyond chatbots.
distractors.E: AI applies across all industries — financial services, energy, travel, retail, healthcare.
distractors.F: Laredo Petroleum proves AI works for physical operations — monitoring wells, detecting leaks, preventing environmental issues.

questions.scenario: A company receives thousands of paper forms daily. They need to digitize the documents and then analyze the text for customer sentiment.
questions.stem: Which combination of AWS services handles document extraction followed by text analysis? (Select TWO)

options.text: Amazon Textract — extracts text, handwriting, and tabular data from scanned documents
Amazon Comprehend — analyzes the extracted text for sentiment, entities, and relationships
Amazon Rekognition — reads text from documents
Amazon Polly — reads extracted text aloud for review
Amazon S3 — automatically analyzes stored documents

explanation.correct: Textract extracts content from documents, then Comprehend analyzes it for insights. These are frequently used together — Textract extracts, Comprehend analyzes the extracted text for sentiment.

distractors.C: Rekognition handles faces/objects in images. Textract specifically handles document extraction (text, forms, tables).
distractors.D: Polly converts text to speech. It doesn't analyze content or extract from documents.
distractors.E: S3 stores documents. It doesn't automatically analyze them. You need Textract + Comprehend.

questions.scenario: A media company wants to make their content more accessible by both creating audio versions of text articles and providing text transcriptions of video content.
questions.stem: Which AWS services handle text-to-speech and speech-to-text? (Select TWO)

options.text: Amazon Polly — text-to-speech synthesis for creating audio from articles
Amazon Transcribe — speech-to-text recognition for creating transcriptions from video/audio
Amazon Translate — converts between text formats
Amazon Comprehend — generates audio summaries
Amazon Lex — records conversations

explanation.correct: Polly (text-to-speech) creates audio from written articles. Transcribe (speech-to-text) creates text transcriptions from video/audio content. Together they bridge text and audio formats.

distractors.C: Translate converts between languages, not between text and speech formats.
distractors.D: Comprehend analyzes text. It doesn't generate audio.
distractors.E: Lex builds conversational interfaces. It doesn't record or transcribe content.

questions.scenario: An enterprise has thousands of internal documents (policies, procedures, technical docs). Employees currently use keyword search that returns too many irrelevant results. They want a system that understands the meaning of questions.
questions.stem: Which AWS service provides intelligent enterprise search using NLP?

options.text: Amazon Kendra — uses ML and NLP to understand natural language questions and respond with intelligently ranked results
Amazon Comprehend — analyzes document sentiment
Amazon OpenSearch — keyword-based search
Amazon S3 — stores documents for retrieval

explanation.correct: Amazon Kendra uses ML and NLP to perform intelligent search of enterprise systems. It understands natural language questions and responds with results based on intelligent understanding, not just keyword matching.

distractors.B: Comprehend analyzes text for insights. Kendra specifically handles intelligent question-answering search.
distractors.C: OpenSearch can do keyword and vector search but Kendra specifically uses NLP to understand questions naturally.
distractors.D: S3 stores documents. It doesn't provide intelligent search or NLP understanding.

questions.scenario: Booking.com's AI Trip Planner engages customers in natural language conversation. When it understands what the customer wants, it calls the booking recommendation API and retrieves customer reviews before making travel recommendations.
questions.stem: What generative AI technique does this AI Trip Planner use?

options.text: Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) — calling external knowledge systems to retrieve information outside the model's training data for more accurate responses
Fine-tuning — retraining the model on booking data every time a customer asks a question
Prompt engineering — using only the prompt without external data
Reinforcement learning — the model learns from customer booking outcomes

explanation.correct: This is RAG — the generative AI model calls external systems (booking API, review database) to retrieve up-to-date information for more accurate, current recommendations. This is what makes gen AI models more accurate and relevant.

distractors.B: The model doesn't retrain for each query. RAG retrieves information at inference time without model modification.
distractors.C: The system goes beyond just prompting — it actively retrieves external data to augment responses.
distractors.D: The system retrieves data in real-time. RL would be a training technique, not an inference-time retrieval process.
